consistency.completeness: The data appears complete for a standard spell entry, including level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, description, and class list.
consistency.naming_conventions: The slug and name match the expected format.
consistency.data_types: All fields use appropriate data types (strings for text, integers for level, arrays for classes).

quality.clarity: The description is very clear, specifying the mechanics (resistance) and the choice available (one of five damage types).
quality.formatting: The formatting of components (V, S) and duration (Concentration, up to 1 hour) follows standard conventions.
quality.relevance: All data points are highly relevant to defining this specific spell.

mechanics.level_school_fit: Level 3 Abjuration is appropriate for a defensive buff granting resistance.
mechanics.casting_time_range_fit: A touch range and 1 action casting time are standard for this type of defensive buff targeting one willing creature.
mechanics.duration_fit: Concentration up to 1 hour suggests an ongoing effect tied to the caster's focus.
mechanics.core_effect_assessment: Grants resistance to one chosen energy type (acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder). This is a powerful but targeted defensive boon, balanced by the concentration requirement.

analysis.recommendations: None. The entry is well-formed and mechanically sound based on standard D&D 5e spell definitions.
